name: Giant Panda
summary: Giant pandas are black-and-white bears from China that eat almost nothing but bamboo. They live in cool mountain forests, spend most of the day feeding, and are famous symbols of wildlife conservation.
metaTitle: Giant Panda Facts | Habitat, Diet, Bamboo & Photos
metaDescription: Discover giant panda facts with photos, bamboo diet, mountain habitat, cub life, and conservation. Learn where pandas live, what they eat, and why they are special.
searchIntents: how big is a giant panda

lifespan.captivity: Up to 30 years in zoos and breeding centers

speed: Slow on ground; strong climber in trees
funFacts: Giant pandas eat bamboo for up to 14 hours a day.
A newborn panda cub is about the size of a stick of butter.
Giant pandas have an extra wrist bone that works like a thumb for holding bamboo.
Despite belonging to the bear family, giant pandas eat almost no meat.
Giant pandas were once found across much of southern and eastern China.
Adult giant pandas mark their home range with scent from glands near their tail.

faq.question: What is a giant panda?
faq.answer: A giant panda is a large black-and-white bear from China. It eats mostly bamboo and lives in cool mountain forests.

faq.question: Where do giant pandas live?
faq.answer: Wild giant pandas live only in mountain forests in central China, mainly in Sichuan, Shaanxi, and Gansu provinces.

faq.question: What do giant pandas eat?
faq.answer: Giant pandas eat almost nothing but bamboo—leaves, shoots, and stems. Bamboo makes up about 99 percent of their diet.

faq.question: Are giant pandas endangered?
faq.answer: Giant pandas are listed as Vulnerable. Their numbers are slowly rising thanks to protected forests and breeding programs.

faq.question: Do giant pandas live in groups?
faq.answer: Adult giant pandas usually live alone. Mothers raise cubs until the young pandas can feed and survive on their own.

faq.question: Why do giant pandas eat bamboo?
faq.answer: Bamboo is available year-round in panda forests. Over time, giant pandas evolved to depend on it as their main food.
